channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. seeks NATO help with Strait of Hormuz. And, federal judge blocks vaccine changes
item.description: As the war with Iran intensifies, Trump is demanding that allies help the U.S. reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And, a federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies.
item.content:encoded:
As the war with Iran intensifies, Trump is demanding that allies help the U.S. reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And, a federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel says it killed 2 top Iranian commanders in a targeted strike
item.description: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani, the highest-profile assassinations since the targeting of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on the first day of the war.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani, the highest-profile assassinations since the targeting of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on the first day of the war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Is there a more fair way to sell World Cup tickets?
item.description: World Cup tickets are expensive, and buying them has been frustrating and confusing. But this is what economics is for: figuring out the best ways to allocate scarce resources. FIFA, steal these ideas.
item.content:encoded: World Cup tickets are expensive, and buying them has been frustrating and confusing. But this is what economics is for: figuring out the best ways to allocate scarce resources. FIFA, steal these ideas.
item.dc:creator: Alex Mayyasi

item.title: I'm concerned about my blood pressure. Can I check it at home?
item.description: If you get a high reading at the doctor's office, it may not be definitive. Here's what to know about your risk — and testing your blood pressure at home.
item.content:encoded: If you get a high reading at the doctor's office, it may not be definitive. Here's what to know about your risk — and testing your blood pressure at home.
item.dc:creator: Mara Gordon

item.title: Bringing marine life back to South Florida's 'forgotten edge'
item.description: Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.
item.content:encoded:
Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.

(Image credit: Nathan Rott)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: From Descartes to punk rock, X has an extraordinary history
item.description: The letter X can be a lot of things: rebellious, mysterious, religious. For this Word of the Week, we examine its origins and many uses.
item.content:encoded:
The letter X can be a lot of things: rebellious, mysterious, religious. For this Word of the Week, we examine its origins and many uses.

(Image credit: jclegg)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: Reproductive health clinics scramble as Title X funding cliff approaches
item.description: Title X is a 56-year-old federal grant program that supports thousands of clinics that provide birth control and STI testing and treatment. Those clinics could face a funding gap because of a Trump administration delay.
item.content:encoded:
Title X is a 56-year-old federal grant program that supports thousands of clinics that provide birth control and STI testing and treatment. Those clinics could face a funding gap because of a Trump administration delay.

(Image credit: Ed Zurga)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: How Trump's Treasury is shifting sanctions to punish his critics and reward friends
item.description: Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.
item.content:encoded:
Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras, Evaristo Sa and Bastien Ohier/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: Chiara Eisner

item.title: Last protester in detention after Trump's campus crackdown has been released
item.description: Leqaa Kordia, a 33-year-old from the West Bank who has lived in New Jersey since 2016, had been held in a U.S. immigration detention center in Texas since last March.
item.content:encoded:
Leqaa Kordia, a 33-year-old from the West Bank who has lived in New Jersey since 2016, had been held in a U.S. immigration detention center in Texas since last March.

(Image credit: Tony Gutierrez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tennessee teens sue Elon Musk's xAI over AI-generated child sexual abuse material
item.description: The three girls say that the nonconsensual nude images were created by a perpetrator who used AI company xAI's image generation tools.
item.content:encoded:
The three girls say that the nonconsensual nude images were created by a perpetrator who used AI company xAI's image generation tools.

(Image credit: Nicolas Tucat)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan
